title: Quick Identification of Herbal Medicine Mixing Uniformity
subcategory: Rapid Testing of Herbal Medicine Routine Indicators
excerpt: Uniformity of content is a necessary prerequisite to ensure the safety, effectiveness, stability, and control of pharmaceutical preparations. It is also an important indicator to evaluate the scientific and rational nature of the preparation process of pharmaceutical preparations
text:
Quick Identification of Herbal Medicine Mixing Uniformity
Uniformity of content is a necessary prerequisite to ensure the safety, effectiveness, stability, and control of pharmaceutical preparations. It is also an important indicator to evaluate the scientific and rational nature of the preparation process of pharmaceutical preparations. The uniformity of the content directly affects the clinical effect of pharmaceutical preparations, and the inspection of the uniformity of the preparation is an important measure to ensure the quality of the preparation. In the case of herbal medicines, the situation is even more complex, as raw materials, crushing, mixing, sieving, granulation, drying, and even process changes all affect the homogeneity of the preparation.
Our company
offers its customers a method for determining the uniformity of the content of herbal preparations, which is fast, simple, and accurate and reflects the mixing of all ingredients in the preparation process to achieve effective control of the production process of the pharmaceutical preparation.

title: Quick Identification of Herbal Medicine Mixing Uniformity
subcategory: Rapid Testing of Herbal Medicine Routine Indicators
excerpt: Uniformity of content is a necessary prerequisite to ensure the safety, effectiveness, stability, and control of pharmaceutical preparations. It is also an important indicator to evaluate the scientific and rational nature of the preparation process of pharmaceutical preparations
text:
offers its customers a method for determining the uniformity of the content of herbal preparations, which is fast, simple, and accurate and reflects the mixing of all ingredients in the preparation process to achieve effective control of the production process of the pharmaceutical preparation.
We use both chemical and physical methods for content uniformity assessment. In practice, the most commonly used chemical methods are high-performance liquid chromatography (HPLC), near-infrared spectroscopy (NIR), ultraviolet-visible spectrophotometry (UV-vis), and fluorescence spectroscopy for trace analysis. Physical methods for determining content uniformity are mainly performed on the superficial surfaces of pharmaceutical products, such as infrared spectroscopic imaging, digital image processing techniques, and infrared thermal image detection techniques. On the other hand, Raman spectroscopy allows photonic probes to probe inside the pharmaceutical formulation.
Examples of chemical analysis methods - NIR

title: Quick Identification of Herbal Medicine Mixing Uniformity
subcategory: Rapid Testing of Herbal Medicine Routine Indicators
excerpt: Uniformity of content is a necessary prerequisite to ensure the safety, effectiveness, stability, and control of pharmaceutical preparations. It is also an important indicator to evaluate the scientific and rational nature of the preparation process of pharmaceutical preparations
text:
We use both chemical and physical methods for content uniformity assessment. In practice, the most commonly used chemical methods are high-performance liquid chromatography (HPLC), near-infrared spectroscopy (NIR), ultraviolet-visible spectrophotometry (UV-vis), and fluorescence spectroscopy for trace analysis. Physical methods for determining content uniformity are mainly performed on the superficial surfaces of pharmaceutical products, such as infrared spectroscopic imaging, digital image processing techniques, and infrared thermal image detection techniques. On the other hand, Raman spectroscopy allows photonic probes to probe inside the pharmaceutical formulation.
Examples of chemical analysis methods - NIR
We use NIR as a method for testing the uniformity of herbal mixtures. NIR is generated when molecular vibrations jump from the ground state to higher energy levels, and it records information about the multiplicity and ensemble frequencies of the fundamental frequency vibrations of chemical bonds in molecules. The NIR spectrum, therefore, reflects the result of superimposing information from multiple substances in the sample and will change if the type of substance or proportion of substances in the mixed sample is changed. During the mixing process, if the sample is not mixed uniformly enough, the proportions of components contained in the sample removed at different locations are different, and this difference can be reflected by the NIR spectrum. This method allows us to make a judgment about the uniformity of the distribution of all substances in a mixed sample. It is fast enough to enable online detection during the mixing process.

Examples of physical analysis methods - digital image processing techniques
Digital image processing is a technique for converting image signals into digital signals and processing them with a computer, including pre-processing (compression, enhancement, recovery) and image segmentation and feature extraction to enable better image recognition. The advantages of this technology are high processing accuracy, good reproducibility, flexibility, etc. We can use digital image processing technology to obtain several horizontal and vertical cross-sections of the herbal mixture and choose the number and location of the components as parameters to establish a reasonable mathematical model to evaluate the overall uniformity of the herbal mixture.

UV standard curve method.
We first prepare a solution of a certain concentration of a standard substance, which is then prepared as a series of standard solutions. The absorbance of each common solution is measured at a certain wavelength, and the standard curve is plotted using the absorbance value as the vertical coordinate and the concentration value of the standard solution as the horizontal coordinate. Finally, the absorbance value of the sample solution is measured according to the standard curve plotting procedure, and the concentration or content of the sample solution is found on the standard curve.

High-performance liquid chromatography (HPLC).
We inject the sample into a polar or non-polar aqueous or organic solvent mobile phase and pass it through a stationary phase column. This column separates the sample components based on chemical properties such as molecular weight, polarity, and charge. We can detect these components by one or more methods, such as UV-visible (UV-vis), mass spectrometry (MS), refractive index, and fluorescence. HPLC can separate compounds regardless of their volatility. The limitation is that no single detector can detect all molecules.

We inject the sample into a polar or non-polar aqueous or organic solvent mobile phase and pass it through a stationary phase column. This column separates the sample components based on chemical properties such as molecular weight, polarity, and charge. We can detect these components by one or more methods, such as UV-visible (UV-vis), mass spectrometry (MS), refractive index, and fluorescence. HPLC can separate compounds regardless of their volatility. The limitation is that no single detector can detect all molecules.
Content determination of different leachates
Water-soluble leachates.
We first crush the test specimen for the assay, then leach and weigh it quickly and precisely using the cold and hot leaching methods. The water-soluble leachate content (%) was calculated on a dry basis.
Alcohol soluble leachate.
Determined according to the water-soluble leachate determination method, using the corresponding concentration of ethanol instead of water as the solvent for each species.
Volatile ether leachate.
Figure 1. Determination procedure for volatile ether leachate content.

Testing of Physical Properties of Herbal Medicines
Herbal medicine has become an essential player in the medical industry and even has obvious therapeutic utility in targeting complications caused by viruses. To ensure the safety and efficacy of herbal medicines,
Our company
provides physical property analysis and testing services to researchers in herbal medicine production, research, development, and application. We are committed to improving the safety and efficacy of herbal medicines by increasing technological innovation and research in herbal medicine testing.
Ash content testing
The Ash content index is an effective control index and a method to check the presence of inorganic drug impurities. Customers can examine the use of raw materials and additives in pharmaceuticals and correctly evaluate the medicinal value of a herb through the ash test we provide. The water-soluble ash and acid-insoluble ash can be used as control indicators in the production of pharmaceuticals.

Testing of General Properties of Herbal Medicines
Any herb has a specific appearance trait due to the genetics of the species. Trait testing is an integral part of herb identification, and there is a correlation between the external characteristics of the herb and its internal quality. Because of the importance of herbal trait testing in ensuring the safety and efficacy of medicines,
Our company
can provide herbal trait identification services to help you determine the authenticity of herbs and obtain specific information about your target herbs.
The introduction of computer technology and digital and pictorial output of results allows us to provide identification characteristics that are no longer subjective, abstract qualitative descriptions but objective, digital indicators, or visual, realistic color maps. And we combine our identification work with histochemical localization techniques so that macroscopic trait identification is linked to microscopic chemical composition analysis. We provide identification results that directly reflect the intrinsic quality of the herbs.
Herb odor characterization detection

title: Testing for Heavy Metals in Herbal Medicines
subcategory: Exogenous Substances Testing of Herbal Medicines
excerpt: Heavy metals are one of the most essential harmful contaminants in herbal medicine, which may be contaminated to varying degrees during production, transport, and storage. Heavy metals such as arsenic (As), chromium (Cd), lead (Pb), mercury (Hg), and copper (Cu) have obvious biot
text:
Testing for Heavy Metals in Herbal Medicines
Heavy metals are one of the most essential harmful contaminants in herbal medicine, which may be contaminated to varying degrees during production, transport, and storage. Heavy metals such as arsenic (As), chromium (Cd), lead (Pb), mercury (Hg), and copper (Cu) have obvious biotoxic effects. They can easily accumulate in the human liver and other organs, causing acute or chronic poisoning in humans, and severe cases, carcinogenic, mutagenic and teratogenic effects.
Accurate determination of heavy metal content in herbs is the key to evaluating heavy metal contamination and the quality of herbs.
Our company
offers a wide range of methods for determining heavy metals such as Pb, Cd, Hg, As, Cu, and other heavy metals in herbs.
Pre-treatment of herbal samples

title: Testing for Heavy Metals in Herbal Medicines
subcategory: Exogenous Substances Testing of Herbal Medicines
excerpt: Heavy metals are one of the most essential harmful contaminants in herbal medicine, which may be contaminated to varying degrees during production, transport, and storage. Heavy metals such as arsenic (As), chromium (Cd), lead (Pb), mercury (Hg), and copper (Cu) have obvious biot
text:
Accurate determination of heavy metal content in herbs is the key to evaluating heavy metal contamination and the quality of herbs.
Our company
offers a wide range of methods for determining heavy metals such as Pb, Cd, Hg, As, Cu, and other heavy metals in herbs.
Pre-treatment of herbal samples
As the digestion of herbal samples is essential for accurately measuring heavy metals, we will recommend the most suitable sample preparation method for your herbal material based on its characteristics and needs. One of the most commonly used pre-treatments for elemental analysis is sample digestion, which destroys the sample's organic matter to release the measured element. The most commonly used methods for sample digestion are dry ashing, wet digestion, and microwave digestion.
Methods for the detection of heavy metals in herbal medicines

title: Testing for Heavy Metals in Herbal Medicines
subcategory: Exogenous Substances Testing of Herbal Medicines
excerpt: Heavy metals are one of the most essential harmful contaminants in herbal medicine, which may be contaminated to varying degrees during production, transport, and storage. Heavy metals such as arsenic (As), chromium (Cd), lead (Pb), mercury (Hg), and copper (Cu) have obvious biot
text:
As the digestion of herbal samples is essential for accurately measuring heavy metals, we will recommend the most suitable sample preparation method for your herbal material based on its characteristics and needs. One of the most commonly used pre-treatments for elemental analysis is sample digestion, which destroys the sample's organic matter to release the measured element. The most commonly used methods for sample digestion are dry ashing, wet digestion, and microwave digestion.
Methods for the detection of heavy metals in herbal medicines
Heavy metals in herbs can be determined by atomic absorption spectrophotometry (AAS), inductively coupled plasma mass spectrometry (ICP-MS), and inductively coupled plasma emission spectrometry (ICP-AES). The atomic absorption spectrophotometric methods include cold atomic absorption, flame atomic absorption spectrophotometry (FAAS ), graphite furnace atomic absorption spectrophotometry (GF-AAS), and hydride-atomic absorption (HG-ASS).
Detection methods
Detection of elements
Preparation of the test solution
Determination conditions

title: Rapid Testing of Herbal Medicine Routine Indicators
excerpt: The quality of herbal medicines is closely related to their efficacy. Traditional methods for controlling the quality of herbal medicines include high-performance liquid chromatography (HPLC), gas chromatography (GC), ultraviolet-visible spectrophotometry (UV-vis), and thin-layer
text:
Rapid Testing of Herbal Medicine Routine Indicators
The quality of herbal medicines is closely related to their efficacy. Traditional methods for controlling the quality of herbal medicines include high-performance liquid chromatography (HPLC), gas chromatography (GC), ultraviolet-visible spectrophotometry (UV-vis), and thin-layer chromatography (TLC). In addition, near-infrared spectroscopy (NIR) and bionanotechnology (bionic smell and taste, electronic eye, fiber optic sensing technology) represent rapid analysis techniques for herbal medicines, which have adapted to the development of herbal medicine research and development and production requirements with their advantages of easy handling, non-destructive samples, no chemical contamination and rapid analysis.
Our company
offers its customers a rapid detection service for routine indicators of herbal medicines with the help of a wide range of methods that enable the rapid detection of indicator components of different herbal samples.
Testing of general properties of herbal medicines

text:
Herbal Rapid Analysis
The complex composition of herbal medicines, traditional analytical methods such as HPLC, GC, GC-MS, HPLC-MS, UV, TLC, etc., require processes such as extraction, separation, purification, and injection of samples, which can achieve the detection of the index components of herbal medicines. Rapid analytical techniques for herbal medicines are highly effective techniques to assist in the development, production, and marketing of herbal medicines, and the methods and techniques are gradually maturing, improving, and moving towards standardization. Rapid analytical techniques for herbal medicines often require no complex sample preparation and only physical information about the sample to be collected to enable rapid qualitative or quantitative analysis.

We use stability testing to examine patterns of change in herbal medicines over time under different environmental conditions to understand and predict trends in the stability of medicines and to support the determination of conditions of manufacture, packaging, storage, and transport of medicines and the determination of expiry dates.
